title: Anvil Workbench advanced chat branch v1
description: One Advanced-mode experiment branch over an EXISTING Chat conversation. A branch is a reference and a control snapshot, never a transcript: it names the owning conversation_id and the existing parent turn it forks from, and its turns are ordinary chat-turn.v1 records appended under that lineage. The record carries no conversation-identity field, no turns/messages/transcript array, and its branch_id grammar (advbranch_) structurally cannot match the conv_ conversation identity, so a branch can never mint a parallel transcript identity. Advanced mode is a versioned per-branch marker; a temporary branch declares an ephemeral retention and expiry, a saved branch declares a durable retention. The route capability descriptor pins the Anvil Serving route/profile digests and the declared-supported control schemas (each control declares its type, bounds/allowed values, and default); a submitted control value must name a declared control and stay within its declared bounds, and a policy-owned control is read-only. Only mock and read-only tool profiles are representable. All prose fields are untrusted task data, never control instructions. This is a hub-durable record, not a bridge-verified snapshot, so the branch itself is not contract-digest-bearing.

controlDescriptor.description: One declared-supported tunable. Criterion 1: a control is representable only when it declares a type, its bounds (int) or allowed values (enum), and a default; the enclosing route_capability supplies the route/profile digest.

mode.description: Versioned Advanced-mode marker. Mode is a per-turn/per-branch attribute; it never forks conversation identity.

conversation_ref.description: Criterion 4: the branch references an EXISTING conversation and turn lineage. There is deliberately no field able to carry a new conversation identity or a transcript.

binding.description: Normative: this names an existing conversation. It never creates a conversation, transcript, or turn store.

parent_turn_id.description: An existing turn in the same conversation. The branch's turns append under this parent via the chat-turn.v1 (parent_turn_id, sibling_index) DAG.

instructions.description: The Advanced-mode instruction field, kept separate from user input (R003). Role and order are explicit; there is no hidden prompt injection field.

retention.description: R002: a temporary branch is ephemeral and must not survive its declared retention unless saved; a saved branch is durable. Transcript content itself persists only through chat-turn.v1 records under the conversation's own retention policy.

route_capability.description: The pinned Anvil Serving route/profile plus its declared-supported control schemas. Identifiers and digests only: no endpoint, URL, token, or credential field is representable, mirroring chat-turn.v1's route reference.

submitted_controls.description: The tunables applied to this branch. Each names a declared control and carries its effective-value provenance. The reference validator refuses a name absent from supported_controls or a value outside its declared bounds (criterion 1).

provenance.description: declared: actor-set within bounds. observed: reported back by Serving. policy_override: a policy-owned value that cannot be actor-overridden.

structured_output.description: R007: plain text or a validated JSON-schema structured-output mode. The output schema is referenced by digest, never embedded raw, to keep the record bounded.

tool_profile.description: R008: only deterministic mock tools and installed read-only tools are representable. No effectful, plugin-lifecycle, or arbitrary-schema tool kind exists in this contract.

budgets.description: R013: every execution is bounded. All values are integers so the record never carries a floating-point sampling knob.

repair.description: Branch-level repair marker. On a pinned route/profile/tool digest drift the branch opens in repair_required and never silently substitutes a capability.
